pull.title: [Perf][DeepSeek] Add sigmoid+bias fusion to fused_grouped_topk from TRTLLM
pull.body:
## Purpose FIX https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/28086 Inspired from [TRTLLM](https://github.com/NVIDIA/TensorRT-LLM/blob/eeb56c2848a23bb60acd82c158d086c4305b249b/cpp/tensorrt_llm/thop/noAuxTcOp.cpp#L37), we add the sigmoid, e_score_correction_bias, and 32bit output cast into the fused_grouped_topk CUDA kernel that we already use for DeepSeek-like models. Gives about a 4.5% speedup on a medium concurrency benchmark with nvfp4 deepseek on B200. Ran a simple dummy profile with `LLM(model="deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1", max_model_len=1024, load_format="dummy", hf_overrides={"num_hidden_layers": 4})` Before: After: ## Test Plan Evals and e2e benchmark ## Test Result Model serve command on 4xB200: ``` vllm serve nvidia/DeepSeek-V3-0324-FP4 -tp 4 ``` ## Accuracy ### Main ``` python tests/evals/gsm8k/gsm8k_eval.py Running GSM8K evaluation: 1319 questions, 5-shot Evaluating: 100%|████████████████████████████████████████████| 1319/1319 [01:27<00:00, 15.10it/s] Results: Accuracy: 0.951 Invalid responses: 0.000 Total latency: 87.365 s Questions per second: 15.098 ``` ### This PR ``` python tests/evals/gsm8k/gsm8k_eval.py Running GSM8K evaluation: 1319 questions, 5-shot Evaluating: 100%|████████████████████████████████████████████| 1319/1319 [01:24<00:00, 15.58it/s] Results: Accuracy: 0.953 Invalid responses: 0.000 Total latency: 84.647 s Questions per second: 15.582 ``` ## Benchmark ### Main ``` vllm bench serve --model nvidia/DeepSeek-V3-0324-FP4 --dataset-name random --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 --random-input-len 512 --random-output-len 128 --num-prompts 128 --max-concurrency 32 --ignore-eos ============ Serving Benchmark Result ============ Successful requests: 128 Failed requests: 0 Maximum request concurrency: 32 Benchmark duration (s): 21.77 Total input tokens: 65408 Total generated tokens: 16384 Request throughput (req/s): 5.88 Output token throughput (tok/s): 752.63 Peak output token throughput (tok/s): 992.00 Peak concurrent requests: 64.00 Total Token throughput (tok/s): 3757.27 ---------------Time to First Token---------------- Mean TTFT (ms): 839.33 Median TTFT (ms): 841.80 P99 TTFT (ms): 1367.84 -----Time per Output Token (excl. 1st token)------ Mean TPOT (ms): 36.15 Median TPOT (ms): 35.02 P99 TPOT (ms): 40.92 ---------------Inter-token Latency---------------- Mean ITL (ms): 36.18 Median ITL (ms): 33.18 P99 ITL (ms): 116.40 ================================================== ``` ### This PR ``` vllm bench serve --model nvidia/DeepSeek-V3-0324-FP4 --dataset-name random --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 --random-input-len 512 --random-output-len 128 --num-prompts 128 --max-concurrency 32 --ignore-eos ============ Serving Benchmark Result ============ Successful requests: 128 Failed requests: 0 Maximum request concurrency: 32 Benchmark duration (s): 20.83 Total input tokens: 65408 Total generated tokens: 16384 Request throughput (req/s): 6.14 Output token throughput (tok/s): 786.43 Peak output token throughput (tok/s): 992.00 Peak concurrent requests: 64.00 Total Token throughput (tok/s): 3926.00 ---------------Time to First Token---------------- Mean TTFT (ms): 699.63 Median TTFT (ms): 678.20 P99 TTFT (ms): 1265.36 -----Time per Output Token (excl. 1st token)------ Mean TPOT (ms): 35.43 Median TPOT (ms): 35.03 P99 TPOT (ms): 40.77 ---------------Inter-token Latency---------------- Mean ITL (ms): 35.45 Median ITL (ms): 33.20 P99 ITL (ms): 100.11 ================================================== ``` ---
Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist - [ ] The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)". - [ ] The test plan, such as providing test command. - [ ] The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results - [ ] (Optional) The necessary documentation update, such as updating `supported_models.md` and `examples` for a new model. - [ ] (Optional) Release notes update. If your change is user facing, please update the release notes draft in the [Google Doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YyVqrgX4gHTtrstbq8oWUImOyPCKSGnJ7xtTpmXzlRs/edit?tab=t.0).

auto_merge.commit_title: [Perf][DeepSeek] Add sigmoid+bias fusion to fused_grouped_topk from TRTLLM (#28124)

files.patch:
@@ -1898,25 +1898,40 @@ def topk_softmax(
 
 def grouped_topk(
     scores: torch.Tensor,
-    scores_with_bias: torch.Tensor,
     num_expert_group: int,
     topk_group: int,
     topk: int,
     renormalize: bool,
     routed_scaling_factor: float,
+    bias: torch.Tensor,
+    scoring_func: int = 0,
 ):
+    """
+    Perform grouped top-k routing for mixture of experts.
+
+    Args:
+        scores: Raw inputs (logits if scoring_func=1, scores if scoring_func=0)
+        num_expert_group: Number of expert groups
+        topk_group: Number of groups to select
+        topk: Number of experts to select per token
+        renormalize: Whether to renormalize the output weights
+        routed_scaling_factor: Scaling factor for routing weights
+        bias: Bias tensor (e_score_correction_bias). Always fused in kernel.
+        scoring_func: 0=none (no activation), 1=sigmoid
+    """
     if not current_platform.is_cuda():
         raise NotImplementedError(
             "The fused grouped_topk kernel is only available on CUDA platforms"
         )
     return torch.ops._moe_C.grouped_topk(
         scores,
-        scores_with_bias,
         num_expert_group,
         topk_group,
         topk,
         renormalize,
         routed_scaling_factor,
+        bias,
+        scoring_func,
     )

@@ -1330,24 +1330,37 @@ def fused_grouped_topk(
 ) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
     assert hidden_states.size(0) == gating_output.size(0), "Number of tokens mismatch"
 
-    if scoring_func == "softmax":
+    if scoring_func == "sigmoid":
+        # Fully fused kernel path for sigmoid
+        topk_values, topk_indices = ops.grouped_topk(
+            gating_output,  # raw logits
+            num_expert_group,
+            topk_group,
+            topk,
+            renormalize,
+            routed_scaling_factor,
+            e_score_correction_bias.to(gating_output.dtype),
+            1,  # scoring_func=1 for sigmoid
+        )
+    elif scoring_func == "softmax":
+        # Apply softmax in Python, then use fused kernel
+        # TODO: Add support for softmax in kernel
         scores = torch.softmax(gating_output, dim=-1)
-    elif scoring_func == "sigmoid":
-        scores = gating_output.sigmoid()
+        topk_values, topk_indices = ops.grouped_topk(
+            scores,  # pre-computed scores
+            num_expert_group,
+            topk_group,
+            topk,
+            renormalize,
+            routed_scaling_factor,
+            e_score_correction_bias.to(gating_output.dtype),
+            0,  # scoring_func=0 (no activation, scores already computed)
+        )
     else:
         raise ValueError(f"Unsupported scoring function: {scoring_func}")
 
-    scores_with_bias = scores + e_score_correction_bias.unsqueeze(0)
-    topk_values, topk_indices = ops.grouped_topk(
-        scores,
-        scores_with_bias.to(scores.dtype),
-        num_expert_group,
-        topk_group,
-        topk,
-        renormalize,
-        routed_scaling_factor,
-    )
-    return topk_values.to(torch.float32), topk_indices.to(torch.int32)
+    # Fused kernel outputs float32 values and int32 indices directly
+    return topk_values, topk_indices
 
 
 def inplace_fused_experts(
